summary: In this episode of This or That with Bhartendra, I’m joined by Mr. Vishesh Gandhi & Mrs. Nisha Gandhi to talk about something more important than products and stock tips: how a couple aligns money decisions, goals, and behavior to build real wealth.

chapters.label: Why couples must align on life goals (not products)
The first 3 years: conflict, adjustment, and building a common page
Starting with small wallets: spending, SIPs, and discipline
When they got serious: comprehensive financial planning
Term insurance & health insurance: why it’s the real foundation
Dividing expenses as a couple: practical method
Handling distractions: crypto, stocks, gold, trends & FOMO
Their portfolio mix: MF, stocks, metals/crypto, ULIPs
Behavioral risk is the biggest risk
Core vs Satellite goals + 80/20 approach to experimentation
What they would do differently if starting again
Advice to young couples: discipline, professionals, communication

tool.label: Become a Crorepati
tool.reason: Turn the idea into a number. See the two roads to your first crore, and which one is faster for you.

seo.title: How This Couple Built a ₹10 Crore Portfolio (Without Chasing Returns)
seo.description: In this episode of This or That with Bhartendra, I’m joined by Mr. Vishesh Gandhi & Mrs. Nisha Gandhi to talk about something more important than products

takeaways.text: The couple flips the usual math to income minus savings equals expenses, so saving happens first and spending fits into whatever is left.
They align on shared life goals before choosing any product, which is what carried them through the conflict of the early years onto a common page.
Term and health insurance come before investing here, treated as the real foundation rather than an afterthought.
They sort goals into core (critical) and satellite (flexible), then cap experiments at 10 to 20 percent so FOMO stays contained without derailing the plan.
They name behavioral risk as the biggest risk, which is why SIPs and goal funding are kept non-negotiable through market noise.
